mission: Get more employees and operational teams onto bikes through a scalable digital platform that connects employers, riders, dealers, financiers, insurers, and service partners.

marketForces: Employer demand for sustainable commuting benefits
Growth of e-bikes and higher-value bike price points
Need for pan-European operational consistency across markets
Margin pressure that makes service, claims, and resale economics important
Expansion through new markets, ecosystem partnerships, and M&A
keyPartners: Captive and third-party leasing or financing entities

designPrinciples: Organize around value streams and shared platforms, not brands alone.
Keep market-facing rollout and partner operations close to country needs, but centralize platform, data, and financial control.
Use platform teams to standardize capabilities that every country and brand needs: identity, integration, workflow, analytics, and compliance.
Treat service, claims, and settlement as product areas with clear ownership, not merely support functions.
Maintain one product and engineering system of record even if commercial labels differ by market.

recommendedStructure.mission: Acquire, launch, and expand employer programs with reusable policy, onboarding, and ROI tooling.
teams: Commercial operations and customer success pods

recommendedStructure.mission: Own rider acquisition, enrollment, app experience, and lifecycle retention.
Operate the ecosystem that keeps bikes delivered, repaired, and riders mobile.
Control lease origination, contracts, settlement, financial integrity, renewals, and remarketing economics.
Build the second growth engine for pooled bikes, campus fleets, and operational use cases.
Provide the shared technical foundation required for scale, acquisition integration, and cross-market leverage.
teams: Data platform and analytics engineering team
Site reliability and security engineering team

recommendedStructure.mission: Ensure coherent strategy, governance, and standards across the full company.

operatingModelNotes: Each value-stream group should own clear product metrics, operating metrics, and a defined P&L contribution logic.
Country operations should sit in market units, but platform product, engineering, architecture, data, and core operations should be centralized.
Brands can remain market-facing, while capability ownership stays global and explicit.
For a 500-person company, 8-12 product-engineering squads plus focused operations and commercial enablement is a pragmatic scale point.
